question.text: In a book-built IPO, the cap price of the price band must fall within which range relative to the floor price?

answer.text: At least 100% and not more than 110% of the floor price
At least 110% and not more than 125% of the floor price
At least 100% and not more than 115% of the floor price
At least 105% and not more than 120% of the floor price

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Under SEBI ICDR Regulations, the cap price in a book-built issue must be at least 105% and not more than 120% of the floor price. This ensures the price band is neither too narrow (less than 5%) nor too wide (more than 20%). **The Trap:** The 110%-125% range sounds plausible but overstates both limits. Many candidates confuse the upper bound of 120% with 125%. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series V-A (Mutual Fund) tests book building awareness when discussing IPO investments by mutual fund schemes. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** When advising retail clients on IPOs, remember that the effective price discovery range is 5%-20% from floor — bids at cut-off price mean accepting any price within this band.
